items.body:
## Summary

This PR fixes several documentation inconsistencies found across the `documentation/` folder by cross-referencing the actual source code and repository structure.

---

### 1. `Quickstart-on-how-to-write-tests.md` — Outdated mocha/chai patterns

Both code examples in this file used mocha and chai, which were replaced by vitest in this repository. Specifically:

- **Example 1 (`glossary.spec.ts`)**: Replaced `import { assert } from "chai"` with vitest imports; replaced `beforeEach(async function() {...})` with `(ctx) =>` arrow function; updated import paths to use `.js` extension (ESM).
- **Example 1 (`utils/recordedClient.ts`)**: Removed `import { Context } from "mocha"`; replaced with `import type { VitestTestContext } from "`@azure-tools/test-recorder`"`; updated `createRecorder(context: Context)` to `createRecorder(context: VitestTestContext)`; changed `new Recorder(context.currentTest)` to `new Recorder(context)`.
- **Example 2 (`monitor.spec.ts`)**: Removed `import { assert } from "chai"` and `import { Context } from "mocha"`; added vitest imports; updated `beforeEach(async function (this: Context)` to `beforeEach(async (ctx) =>`; updated `new Recorder(this.currentTest)` to `new Recorder(ctx)`.

These patterns now match the actual test files in the repository (e.g., `sdk/purview/purview-workflow-rest/test/public/workflow.spec.ts`, `sdk/fabric/arm-fabric/test/public/utils/recordedClient.ts`).

---

### 2. `Generate-code-from-TypeSpec.md` — Wrong pnpm package name

The `code-gen-pipeline` prerequisites section had `npm install -g `@pnpm`` which is an invalid package name. The correct package name for pnpm is just `pnpm` (no `@` scope prefix).

**Before:** `npm install -g `@pnpm``  
**After:** `npm install -g pnpm`

---

### 3. `steps-after-generations.md` — Missing `...` on build filter commands

Three `pnpm build --filter=${PACKAGE_NAME}` commands were missing the trailing `...` which is required to also build the package's dependencies (per the repo's CONTRIBUTING.md and CLAUDE.md). The Linux variant had the `...` but the Windows variants did not, and one Linux playback variant was also missing it.

All three instances on lines 70, 79, and 85 have been fixed to include the trailing `...`.

---

### 4. `using-azure-identity.md` — Stale `@azure/identity` information

**`InteractiveBrowserCredential`**:
- The doc described it as following the "implicit grant flow", which Microsoft has deprecated. The actual `@azure/identity` v3+ implementation uses the authorization code flow with PKCE (`sdk/identity/identity/src/credentials/interactiveBrowserCredential.ts:127`).
- The doc stated "this credential can only be used in the browser but Node.js support will be added in the future" — this was fixed years ago. `InteractiveBrowserCredential` fully supports Node.js (opens a browser window).
- Removed instructions to enable "Implicit grant" in app registrations, which is no longer the correct setup.

**`DefaultAzureCredential`**:
- The doc listed only `EnvironmentCredential` and `ManagedIdentityCredential` in the chain. The actual implementation (`sdk/identity/identity/src/credentials/defaultAzureCredential.ts`) tries 7+ credential types: `EnvironmentCredential`, `WorkloadIdentityCredential`, `ManagedIdentityCredential`, `VisualStudioCodeCredential`, `AzureCliCredential`, `AzurePowerShellCredential`, and `AzureDeveloperCliCredential`.
items.title: Fix documentation inconsistencies in documentation/ folder
